circuit:Abu Dhabi.title: Yas Marina Circuit
circuit:Abu Dhabi.summary: The Yas Marina Circuit on Yas Island in Abu Dhabi spans 5. 281 kilometers and was designed by Hermann Tilke to serve as the venue for Formula One's season finale. Since its debut in 2009, the track has become the final round of the championship calendar and represents one of four Middle Eastern circuits in Formula One racing.

circuit:Australia.title: Albert Park Circuit
circuit:Australia.summary: Albert Park's street circuit winds around a Melbourne lake and hosts the annual Formula One Australian Grand Prix, along with supporting racing categories. The track holds FIA Grade 1 certification for international motorsport competition.

circuit:Austria.title: Red Bull Ring
circuit:Austria.summary: Located in Spielberg, Styria, Austria, this 4. 326 km circuit originally opened as the Österreichring and was the home of the Austrian Grand Prix throughout the 1970s and 1980s. After undergoing reconstruction and shortening, the track was renamed and returned to hosting the Grand Prix from 1997 until the early 2000s.

circuit:Azerbaijan.title: Baku City Circuit
circuit:Azerbaijan.summary: The Baku City Circuit is a street track in Azerbaijan's capital city, positioned along Baku Boulevard with a lap length of 6. 003 km, ranking it among the lengthier venues on the Formula One calendar. The circuit first hosted a Formula One race as the European Grand Prix in 2016, before being renamed the Azerbaijan Grand Prix from 2017 onwards.

circuit:Bahrain.title: Bahrain International Circuit
circuit:Bahrain.summary: Opened in 2004, the Bahrain International Circuit is a 5. 412 km venue that hosted the inaugural Formula One race in the Middle East that same year, and continues to hold the annual Bahrain Grand Prix alongside other motorsport categories. The FIA Grade 1-licensed facility features multiple configurations and has accommodated various racing series including GP2, drag racing, and endurance competitions, though Australian V8 Supercars competed there only from 2006 to 2010.

circuit:Barcelona.title: Circuit de Barcelona-Catalunya
circuit:Barcelona.summary: Located in Montmeló near Barcelona, this 4. 657 km circuit in Catalonia features a balanced blend of extended straights and diverse corner configurations, making it a comprehensive test of racing performance. With FIA Grade 1 certification and spectator capacity exceeding 140,000, the track hosts prestigious motorsport events throughout the year.

circuit:Belgium.title: Circuit de Spa-Francorchamps
circuit:Belgium.summary: The Belgian circuit spanning 7. 004 km near Stavelot has been a Formula One fixture since 1950, hosting the championship's inaugural season. After a brief hiatus in 2003 and 2006, the venue has maintained an uninterrupted presence on the F1 calendar annually, establishing itself as a foundational Grand Prix location with roots tracing back to 1925.

circuit:Canada.title: Circuit Gilles Villeneuve
circuit:Canada.summary: Located on Notre Dame Island in Montreal, Quebec, the Circuit Gilles Villeneuve is a 4. 361 km temporary road course that serves as the home of the Formula One Canadian Grand Prix. Beyond Formula One, the venue has accommodated numerous racing series including sportscar championships, open-wheel competitions, and stock car racing events.

circuit:China.title: Shanghai International Circuit
circuit:China.summary: The Shanghai International Circuit, located in the Jiading District of Shanghai, is a motorsport facility most recognized for hosting the Chinese Grand Prix, which ran annually from 2004 to 2019 before resuming in 2024. The track also began hosting the Shanghai ePrix from 2024 onwards.

circuit:Great Britain.title: Silverstone Circuit
circuit:Great Britain.summary: Silverstone, located in Northamptonshire, England, is the traditional home of the British Grand Prix, having inaugurated the event in 1948 and hosting the inaugural World Championship race in 1950. After decades of alternating with other British venues, the Grand Prix became permanently based at Silverstone from 1987 onwards, while the circuit also stages MotoGP competitions.

circuit:Hungary.title: Hungaroring
circuit:Hungary.summary: The Hungaroring, a 4. 381 km circuit near Budapest in Mogyoród, hosted the first Formula One Grand Prix ever held in Eastern Europe when it opened in 1986. Built in just eight months, faster than any other Formula One venue, the track was constructed near a major highway after the Hungarian government rejected plans for a street circuit in Budapest's central park.

circuit:Italy.title: Autodromo Nazionale Monza
circuit:Italy.summary: The Autodromo Nazionale Monza, located near Milan in northern Italy, is a 5. 793 km circuit constructed in 1922 that ranks among the world's earliest purpose-built racing venues. Home to the Italian Grand Prix since 1949 (except during a 1980 closure for renovation), the track has earned the nickname "Temple of Speed" for its distinctive combination of extended straights and fast-flowing corners.

circuit:Japan.title: Suzuka International Racing Course
circuit:Japan.summary: Suzuka Circuit, situated in Mie Prefecture, Japan, is a 5. 807 km motorsports facility operated by Honda Mobilityland with a spectator capacity of 155,000. The track is primarily renowned for hosting Formula One World Championship races and serves as a prominent venue in the Japanese Super Formula series.

circuit:Las Vegas.title: Las Vegas Strip Circuit
circuit:Las Vegas.summary: The Las Vegas Strip Circuit is a street course that navigates through Paradise, Nevada, following sections of Las Vegas Boulevard and incorporating the region's most recognizable venues such as MSG Sphere, Caesars Palace, Bellagio, and Paris Las Vegas. The layout winds through the city streets in proximity to the famous Las Vegas Strip, an area renowned for its concentration of major hotels and casinos.

circuit:Mexico City.title: Autódromo Hermanos Rodríguez
circuit:Mexico City.summary: Located in Mexico City, the Autódromo Hermanos Rodríguez is a 4. 304 km circuit honoring two racing brothers, Ricardo and Pedro Rodríguez, both of whom died in racing accidents during the 1960s and early 1970s. The venue has hosted the Mexican Grand Prix across multiple eras, returning to the Formula One calendar in 2015 on its current layout after a 23-year absence, with the event now branded as the Mexico City Grand Prix since 2021.

circuit:Miami.title: Miami International Autodrome
circuit:Miami.summary: The Miami International Autodrome is a temporary circuit constructed around Hard Rock Stadium in Miami Gardens, featuring a 5. 412 km layout with 19 corners that produces average qualifying speeds near 224 km/h. Designed specifically by Apex Circuit Design for Formula One racing, the venue joined the championship calendar in 2022 as the home of the Miami Grand Prix.

circuit:Monaco.title: Circuit de Monaco
circuit:Monaco.summary: The Circuit de Monaco is a 3. 337 km street track that winds through the Monte Carlo and La Condamine districts of Monaco, encompassing the harbor area. Though technically spanning multiple neighborhoods, the circuit is widely known as Monte Carlo since the majority of its layout occupies that particular district.

circuit:Netherlands.title: Circuit Zandvoort
circuit:Netherlands.summary: Located in the Dutch coastal dunes near Amsterdam, the Zandvoort circuit is a 4. 259 km track that resumed hosting Formula One in 2021 after a lengthy absence, bringing back the Dutch Grand Prix to the calendar. The venue's partnership with Formula One is scheduled to conclude following the 2026 season.

circuit:Qatar.title: Lusail International Circuit
circuit:Qatar.summary: The Lusail International Circuit is a Qatari racing facility positioned near Lusail, north of Doha, spanning 5. 419 kilometers with a 1. 068-kilometer main straight.

circuit:São Paulo.title: Autódromo José Carlos Pace
circuit:São Paulo.summary: Interlagos is a 4. 309 km counterclockwise racing circuit in São Paulo, Brazil, originally opened in 1940 and later renamed in 1985 to commemorate Formula One driver José Carlos Pace following his fatal plane crash in 1977. The venue hosts major motorsport events and contains an adjacent kart track dedicated to fellow Brazilian racing legend Ayrton Senna.

circuit:Saudi Arabia.title: Jeddah Corniche Circuit
circuit:Saudi Arabia.summary: The Jeddah Corniche Circuit is a street track situated along the Red Sea waterfront in Jeddah, Saudi Arabia, which has been the venue for the Formula One Saudi Arabian Grand Prix since 2021.

circuit:Singapore.title: Marina Bay Street Circuit
circuit:Singapore.summary: The Marina Bay Street Circuit winds through Singapore's downtown waterfront area, incorporating the Downtown Core and Kallang districts in its layout.

circuit:Spain.title: Circuit de Barcelona-Catalunya
circuit:Spain.summary: Located near Barcelona in northeastern Spain, the Circuit de Barcelona-Catalunya stretches 4. 657 kilometers and features a balanced layout combining extended straights with diverse turn configurations, making it suitable for testing multiple racing disciplines. The venue holds FIA Grade 1 certification and accommodates approximately 140,700 spectators across its grandstands.

circuit:United States.title: Circuit of the Americas
circuit:United States.summary: Located near Austin, Texas, the Circuit of the Americas is a 3. 426-mile international-standard racing facility that serves as the permanent home of the Formula One United States Grand Prix. The venue also hosts major motorcycle racing competitions and endurance racing events, while having previously accommodated various other racing series including IndyCar and sports car championships.

driver:ALB.title: Alexander Albon
driver:ALB.summary: Alexander Albon is a dual Thai and British racing driver currently competing for Williams in Formula One, representing Thailand on the grid.

driver:ALO.title: Fernando Alonso
driver:ALO.summary: Spanish driver Fernando Alonso represents Aston Martin in Formula One, bringing two world championship titles earned with Renault in 2005 and 2006 alongside 32 Grand Prix victories accumulated over 23 seasons. Beyond F1, he has achieved significant success in endurance motorsport, including two Le Mans 24 Hours victories with Toyota and the 2018–19 World Endurance Championship, making him the sole driver to win both the F1 and World Endurance championships.

driver:ANT.title: Andrea Kimi Antonelli
driver:ANT.summary: Italian driver Andrea Kimi Antonelli began his Formula One career in 2025 and currently races for Mercedes, having claimed five Grand Prix victories to date.

driver:BEA.title: Oliver Bearman
driver:BEA.summary: Oliver Bearman is a British racing driver currently competing in Formula One with the Haas team.

driver:BOR.title: Gabriel Bortoleto
driver:BOR.summary: Brazilian driver Gabi Bortoleto currently races for Audi in Formula One, following his rise through international motorsport competition.

driver:BOT.title: Valtteri Bottas
driver:BOT.summary: Valtteri Bottas, a Finnish driver, currently races for Cadillac in Formula One after a lengthy career spanning 13 seasons. The veteran has achieved two runner-up finishes in the drivers' championship while with Mercedes and has secured 10 Grand Prix victories throughout his time in the sport.

driver:COL.title: Franco Colapinto
driver:COL.summary: Franco Alejandro Colapinto is an Argentine motorsport competitor currently racing in Formula One with the Alpine team.

driver:DOO.title: Jack Doohan (racing driver)
driver:DOO.summary: Australian driver Jack Doohan holds a reserve role at Haas in Formula One while competing in endurance racing for Nielsen Racing in the LMP2 category. He has appeared in seven Grand Prix events during the 2024-2025 season.

driver:GAS.title: Pierre Gasly
driver:GAS.summary: Pierre Gasly is a French Formula One driver currently racing for Alpine. The 2020 Italian Grand Prix victory represents a significant career highlight, which he achieved while driving for AlphaTauri.

driver:HAD.title: Isack Hadjar
driver:HAD.summary: Isack Hadjar is a French-Algerian driver competing for Red Bull Racing in Formula One under French nationality.

driver:HAM.title: Lewis Hamilton
driver:HAM.summary: Lewis Hamilton is a British Formula One driver currently racing for Ferrari, holding an unprecedented seven world championship titles that he shares with Michael Schumacher. The driver has set multiple records throughout his career, including the most race victories, pole positions, and podium finishes in the sport's history.

driver:HUL.title: Nico Hülkenberg
driver:HUL.summary: Nicolas Hülkenberg, a German driver currently racing for Audi in Formula One, achieved success beyond the series by claiming victory in the 24 Hours of Le Mans in 2015 while driving for Porsche.

driver:LAW.title: Liam Lawson
driver:LAW.summary: Liam Lawson is a New Zealand-born racing driver currently competing in Formula One with the Racing Bulls team.

driver:LEC.title: Charles Leclerc
driver:LEC.summary: The Monégasque driver currently races for Ferrari in Formula One, having secured nine Grand Prix victories throughout his career. Leclerc came closest to the world championship in 2022 when he finished second in the drivers' standings while competing for the Italian team.

driver:MAG.title: Kevin Magnussen
driver:MAG.summary: Kevin Magnussen is a Danish driver who has competed in Formula One across two separate periods, from 2014-2020 and 2022-2024. Currently, he races in the World Endurance Championship for BMW-affiliated WRT while maintaining a part-time NASCAR presence with Trackhouse Racing.

driver:NOR.title: Lando Norris
driver:NOR.summary: Lando Norris, a British driver competing for McLaren, claimed the 2025 Formula One World Drivers' Championship with the team. Across his eight seasons in the sport, he has secured victories in 11 Grand Prix races.

driver:OCO.title: Esteban Ocon
driver:OCO.summary: Esteban Ocon is a French Formula One driver currently racing for Haas. The 2021 Hungarian Grand Prix victory with Alpine remains a significant milestone in his career.

driver:PER.title: Sergio Pérez
driver:PER.summary: Mexican driver Sergio Pérez competes for Cadillac in Formula One, bringing extensive experience from 15 seasons in the sport. He finished as runner-up in the 2023 drivers' championship while racing for Red Bull and has secured six race victories throughout his career.

driver:PIA.title: Oscar Piastri
driver:PIA.summary: Oscar Piastri, an Australian driver, competes for McLaren in Formula One and has secured nine Grand Prix victories during his four seasons in the sport.

driver:RIC.title: Daniel Ricciardo
driver:RIC.summary: Australian driver Daniel Ricciardo competed in Formula One for 14 seasons until 2024, securing eight Grand Prix victories during his career in the sport. Known by his nickname "the Honey Badger," Ricciardo was a fixture on the grid from 2011 onwards.

driver:RUS.title: George Russell (racing driver)
driver:RUS.summary: George Russell, a British driver, has secured seven victories during his eight seasons competing in Formula One, currently racing for Mercedes.

driver:SAI.title: Carlos Sainz Jr.
driver:SAI.summary: Spanish driver Carlos Sainz competes for Williams in Formula One, where he has accumulated four Grand Prix victories throughout his 12-season career in the sport.

driver:SAR.title: Logan Sargeant
driver:SAR.summary: Logan Sargeant is an American driver currently racing in the FIA World Endurance Championship's LMGT3 category with Proton Competition, following his previous stint in IMSA's LMP2 class. He spent two seasons competing in Formula One between 2023 and 2024 before transitioning to sports car racing.

driver:STR.title: Lance Stroll
driver:STR.summary: Lance Stroll is a Canadian Formula One driver currently racing for Aston Martin in the sport's premier motorsport category.

driver:TSU.title: Yuki Tsunoda
driver:TSU.summary: Yuki Tsunoda is a Japanese racing driver who competed in Formula One between 2021 and 2025, currently holding a reserve driver position with Red Bull Racing and Racing Bulls.

driver:VER.title: Max Verstappen
driver:VER.summary: The Dutch-Belgian driver races for Red Bull Racing under the Dutch flag and has secured four consecutive world championships between 2021 and 2024. With 71 Grand Prix victories accumulated across 12 seasons, Verstappen stands as one of Formula One's most successful drivers.

driver:ZHO.title: Zhou Guanyu
driver:ZHO.summary: Zhou Guanyu is China's sole Formula One competitor, having raced in the sport from 2022 through 2024. He currently holds a reserve driver position with Cadillac in the championship.

team:Alpine.title: Alpine F1 Team
team:Alpine.summary: Alpine Racing operates from Enstone as Renault's works Formula One team, competing under a French licence since being rebranded from Renault F1 Team in 2021 to promote the Alpine sports car brand. The squad achieved one victory in its Alpine incarnation when Esteban Ocon won the 2021 Hungarian Grand Prix, before Renault's withdrawal from the sport at the conclusion of 2025. Currently powered by Mercedes engines, the team fields Pierre Gasly and Franco Colapinto as its driver pairing.

team:Aston Martin.title: Aston Martin in Formula One
team:Aston Martin.summary: Aston Martin, a British automotive manufacturer, has maintained a presence in Formula One across multiple eras, with its modern incarnation operating as AMR GP Limited under the Aston Martin Aramco Formula One Team designation.

team:Ferrari.title: Scuderia Ferrari
team:Ferrari.summary: Scuderia Ferrari HP represents the racing operations of the Italian luxury automobile manufacturer and stands as Formula One's longest-running and most accomplished team, participating continuously in the World Championship from its inception in 1950. The squad carries the nickname "the Prancing Horse," derived from their distinctive emblem, and has established itself as a dominant force throughout the sport's history.

team:Haas F1 Team.title: Haas F1 Team
team:Haas F1 Team.summary: Gene Haas established an American-licensed Formula One team in 2014, which competed in its first season during 2016. Ayao Komatsu currently leads the team as principal, having taken over from Guenther Steiner, who had held the position since the team's founding before stepping down in early 2024.

team:Kick Sauber.title: Sauber Motorsport
team:Kick Sauber.summary: The Swiss engineering outfit founded by Peter Sauber in 1970 initially made its mark in endurance racing before joining Formula One as an independent constructor in 1993. Operating for over three decades in the sport, the team established itself as one of the longest-serving chassis builders in the championship's history before its acquisition by Audi in 2024 and subsequent departure from the grid in 2025.

team:McLaren.title: McLaren
team:McLaren.summary: McLaren Racing Limited is a British Formula One outfit headquartered at its technology facility in Woking, Surrey. Operating as a subsidiary of the McLaren Group, the team competes under Bahraini ownership through Mumtalakat Holding Company.

team:Mercedes.title: Mercedes-AMG Petronas F1 Team
team:Mercedes.summary: Mercedes has competed in Formula One since 1954 as both a constructor and engine supplier, with its current operation based in Brackley as Mercedes-AMG Petronas. The team adopted a tripartite ownership structure in January 2022, with equal stakes held by Mercedes-Benz Group, team principal Toto Wolff, and Ineos. The squad carries the storied "Silver Arrows" designation in the sport.

team:Racing Bulls.title: Racing Bulls
team:Racing Bulls.summary: Racing Bulls is an Italian Formula One team and constructor that entered the sport in 2024 as part of Red Bull's two-team operation. Based in Faenza with additional facilities in Milton Keynes, the squad operates as a sister team to Red Bull Racing under Austrian parent company Red Bull GmbH.

team:RB.title: Racing Bulls
team:RB.summary: Visa Cash App Racing Bulls is an Italian Formula One team and constructor that commenced competition in 2024, operating as one of two squads under Red Bull's ownership alongside Red Bull Racing. Headquartered in Faenza with a secondary facility near Milton Keynes, the team represents Red Bull's second racing division in the championship.

team:Red Bull Racing.title: Red Bull Racing
team:Red Bull Racing.summary: Oracle Red Bull Racing, operating under an Austrian licence from its English base, emerged as a Formula One competitor in 2005 and became one of two top-tier racing operations within the Red Bull GmbH portfolio alongside Racing Bulls. The team experienced significant stability under Christian Horner's leadership spanning two decades before Laurent Mekies assumed the leadership role in 2025.

team:Williams.title: Williams Racing
team:Williams.summary: Williams Grand Prix Engineering is a British Formula One constructor founded in 1977 by Frank Williams and Patrick Head, succeeding Williams's earlier failed racing venture. Based in Grove, Oxfordshire, the team competes in 2026 under the Atlassian Williams F1 Team name and operates from a substantial facility in the English Midlands.
